question: Using FY2025 Connectivity segment figures, calculate YoY growth in revenue, operating income, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA. What do the differential growth rates imply about operating leverage within Connectivity?
facts: Connectivity revenue was $11,387M in FY2025
Connectivity revenue was $7,599M in FY2024
Connectivity revenue grew 49.8% YoY
Connectivity income from operations was $4,423M in FY2025
Connectivity income from operations was $2,006M in FY2024
Connectivity income from operations grew 120.5% YoY
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $7,168M in FY2025
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,849M in FY2024
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA grew 86.2% YoY
Operating income grew at 2.42× the rate of revenue
Segment Adjusted EBITDA grew at 1.73× the rate of revenue
Operating margin expanded from 26.40% to 38.84% (+1,244 bps)
Segment Adj. EBITDA margin expanded from 50.65% to 62.95% (+1,230 bps)
Incremental operating margin was 63.81%
Incremental Adj. EBITDA margin was 87.62%
Cost of revenue grew only 24.2% vs. 49.8% revenue growth
D&A grew 57.6% (+$868M), faster than revenue, reflecting capital deployment for new satellite launches
The gap between operating income growth (+120.5%) and EBITDA growth (+86.2%) signals growth-phase capital intensity
The Connectivity segment exhibits strong and accelerating operating leverage characteristic of a scale-driven network infrastructure business

question: Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.
facts: Connectivity Segment Revenue was $3,257M in Q1 2026
Products Revenue (Starlink Kit Sales) was $380M in Q1 2026
Connectivity Service Revenue was $2,877M in Q1 2026
Starlink Subscribers at end of Q1 2026 was 10.3M
Starlink Subscribers at beginning of Q1 2026 was 8.9M
Average Starlink Subscribers was 9.6M in Q1 2026
Implied ARPU using Total Connectivity Revenue was $113.09/month in Q1 2026
Implied ARPU using Connectivity Service Revenue was $99.90/month in Q1 2026
Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU was $66/month in Q1 2026
The implied ARPU of $99.90/month overstates the disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month by approximately $34/month (51%)
The estimate assumes average subscriber count equals the simple midpoint of beginning-of-period and end-of-period subscribers
The estimate assumes Q1 2026 beginning subscribers equal FY2025 ending subscribers (8.9M)
The estimate assumes product revenue is excluded from the service-revenue-based ARPU calculation
Managed enterprise customers with direct, negotiated agreements through the Starlink sales team are excluded from the Starlink Subscriber count
Government/Starshield customers under long-term, multi-year contracts are excluded from the Starlink Subscriber count
The Starlink Subscriber count reflects only unique Service Lines tied to a consumer-facing Starlink.com account
The Starlink Subscriber count encompasses Personal plans (Residential, Roam) and Business plans (Local Priority, Global Priority)
Enterprise & Government service revenue ($976M in Q1 2026) is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator
Starlink Mobile (satellite-to-mobile) revenue from MNO revenue-sharing arrangements is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator
Product revenue ($380M in Q1 2026) is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator
A Starlink Subscriber equals 1 Service Line, not 1 person

question: Calculate AI and Connectivity segment capex intensity for FY2025 and Q1 2026. Compare quarter versus full-year intensity and assess what the change implies about the pace and stage of AI infrastructure buildout.
facts: AI capex was $12,727M in FY2025
AI capex was $7,723M in Q1 2026
AI revenue was $3,201M in FY2025
AI revenue was $818M in Q1 2026
Connectivity capex was $4,178M in FY2025
Connectivity capex was $1,332M in Q1 2026
Connectivity revenue was $11,387M in FY2025
Connectivity revenue was $3,257M in Q1 2026
AI capex intensity was 397.6% in FY2025
AI capex intensity was 944.1% in Q1 2026
Connectivity capex intensity was 36.7% in FY2025
Connectivity capex intensity was 40.9% in Q1 2026
AI capex intensity increased by 546.5 pp from FY2025 to Q1 2026
Connectivity capex intensity increased by 4.2 pp from FY2025 to Q1 2026
Q1 2026 AI capex represents 60.7% of FY2025 AI capex
SpaceX has moved from an initial investment ramp into an aggressive, peak-capex buildout phase for AI infrastructure
The company is deep in the pre-revenue scaling phase of AI infrastructure
The current investment pattern is characteristic of early-to-mid stage infrastructure buildout where economic returns will only become visible with a multi-year lag
Connectivity exhibits a capital-light profile typical of an established, cash-generating segment
The increase in Connectivity capex intensity from 36.7% to 40.9% reflects continued but disciplined network expansion, not a disruptive investment phase
